com.mentor.chs.plugin.harnessimport

Interface IXSignalNameConfiguration

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXPlugin, IXSignalNameGenerator

    public interface IXSignalNameConfiguration
extends IXSignalNameGenerator
    Configuration part of a signal name generation plugin for imported harness wires.

    When the user selects a plugin, in the harness import dialog, the getConfigPanel() method is called and the returned panel is added to the lower part of the harness import dialog. The panel can be used to obtain configuration information from the user for the signal name generation plugin. The user may switch between plugins and each time a plugin is selected the getConfigPanel() method will be called.

    There are two alternative mechanisms for the plugin to indicate that the configuration is not valid:

    1. Simple. When the user presses the Import button on the import dialog, the selected plugin's isConfigValid() method will be called. This allows the plugin to indicate to the framework that it is not ready to generate signal names (normally because the user has entered invalid information into the configuration panel). If isConfigValid() returns false the framework will display a message telling the user to check the configuration parameters. In this case, the import action will not proceed.

    2. Dynamic. A listener IXConfigurationListener is provided by the framework so that the plugin can indicate its state as either valid or invalid as the user makes changes to the configuration panel. This allows the framework to enable or disable the import button based on the plugin's state. If this mechanism is implemented, the plugin will typically inform the user of specific issues by using tooltips on the components in the configuration panel. As the import button will only be enabled when the plugin indicates the configuration panel is in a valid state, the plugin should return true from the method isConfigValid().

    Regardless of the state of the configuration panel as indicated either by the listener or by isConfigValid(), the user will still be able to cancel the import harness dialog and the plugin will not be able to veto this action.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void deregisterListener()
      Deregister state changed listener.
      javax.swing.JPanel getConfigPanel()
      Return a Swing JPanel that will be incorporated into the Harness import selection dialog.
      boolean isConfigValid()
      Return true if the plugin has all the information it needs to generate signal names.
      void registerListener(IXConfigurationListener listener)
      Register state changed listener.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.harnessimport.IXSignalNameGenerator

        endHarness, getSignalName, startHarness

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getName, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • getConfigPanel

        javax.swing.JPanel getConfigPanel()
        Return a Swing JPanel that will be incorporated into the Harness import selection dialog. The panel allows the plugin to capture configuration information from the user.
        Returns:
        a panel to be added to the harness import dialog.

      • isConfigValid

        boolean isConfigValid()
        Return true if the plugin has all the information it needs to generate signal names. This method will be called after getConfigPanel() has been called and can be used by the plugin to indicate that the user has entered invalid information into the configuration panel and that the import dialog should not close.
        Returns:
        true if the configuration information is valid

      • registerListener

        void registerListener(IXConfigurationListener listener)
        Register state changed listener. This method will be called after getConfigPanel() is called. The listener should be informed of any change in configuration state.

        Once the listener has been registered the plugin should tell the listener its current state, otherwise the framework will assume that its state is valid.

        Parameters:
        listener - to inform of state changes, this will be the framework.

      • deregisterListener

        void deregisterListener()
        Deregister state changed listener. After this method is call no events should be sent to the listener. This method will be called on the existing plugin when a new plugin is selected or when the import dialog is closed.
